provenance.source_corpus: ALTERNATIVE SILVICULTURAL SYSTEMS FOR HARVESTING AND REGENERATING SPRUCE-DOMINATED BOREAL MINERAL WETLANDS

source_spans.text: Mounding, the chosen site preparation, was carried out on both the strip-cut and the patch-cut areas
There has been better survival on mounded than on non-mounded microsites
Two harvesting systems were tested: narrow clear-cut strips alternating with uncut strips and a patch clear-cut.
Two harvesting systems were tested: narrow clear-cut strips alternating with uncut strips and a patch clear-cut.
Consequently, anoxia is probably the most likely cause of seedling mortality during the first growing season after planting.
Consequently, anoxia is probably the most likely cause of seedling mortality during the first growing season after planting.
The cutting pattern in the strip-cut area kept the wind below the threshold for large-scale windthrow
The cutting pattern in the strip-cut area kept the wind below the threshold for large-scale windthrow
The sunny (south-facing) aspect was almost 1°C warmer for every 100 W × m −2 of additional solar radiation
The sunny (south-facing) aspect was almost 1°C warmer for every 100 W × m −2 of additional solar radiation
Mounding resulted in less cover, especially for the grass stratum
On the patch-cut, where the water table was closest to the surface, seedlings may succumb to anoxia.

edges.original_sentence: There has been better survival on mounded than on non-mounded microsites, in both the strip-cut and the patch-cut, for all species except black spruce in the patch-cut.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:a5638d135c84
edges.original_sentence: Fifth-year seedling survival was significantly better for all species in the strip-cut area than in the patch-cut area.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:7ad5330ec38e
edges.original_sentence: The better survival rates on the mounds may be attributed to the seedlings being raised above the anoxic conditions that existed below ground level in 1997.

edges.original_sentence: The cutting pattern in the strip-cut area kept the wind below the threshold for large-scale windthrow, and the residual strips buffered wind effects across the alternating strips.
